summary: Surah Al-Ma'arij warns of a punishment sought by the deniers, describing a Day that feels fifty thousand years long, with the sky like molten metal and mountains like wool. It exposes human impatience, greed, and panic—except for those who are steadfast in prayer, give the due share to beggars and the deprived, believe in the Hereafter, guard chastity, honor trusts and covenants, uphold truthful testimony, and maintain their prayers. It rebukes the arrogant wish to enter Paradise without faith and reminds that Allah can replace the deniers and will gather all on the promised Day.
